checks.name: trigger_validity
checks.detail: 2 triggers: bi-weekly Friday 5PM schedule + manual trigger

dimensions.name: Prompt Completeness
dimensions.rationale: Identity >50 words. Instructions >200 words with 5 phases. Tool guidance with Jira agile REST, Confluence content API, Slack postMessage specs. Three examples (standard close, zero completions, manual mid-sprint). Error handling. Three custom sections for Confluence template, velocity tracking, and required configuration.

dimensions.name: Tool-Prompt Alignment
dimensions.rationale: http_request mapped to Jira agile REST v1, Confluence REST API, Slack Web API. file_read/write for sprint_velocity.json. No phantom tools.

dimensions.name: Trigger Coherence
dimensions.rationale: Bi-weekly Friday 5PM schedule + manual trigger. Cron expression 0 17 */14 * 5 is questionable - */14 applied to day-of-month may not reliably produce bi-weekly Friday behavior.

dimensions.name: Connector Coverage
dimensions.rationale: Jira, Confluence, Slack all have complete connector definitions. Confluence shares Atlassian credentials with Jira as documented.

dimensions.name: Memory Design
dimensions.rationale: sprint_velocity.json for rolling velocity tracking with 3-sprint averages. Documented in custom section with deviation alerting (>20%).

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.rationale: Jira 401/404/429 with pagination handling, Confluence title conflicts with update fallback, Slack errors, data quality issues (missing story points, empty descriptions), partial failure recovery.

dimensions.name: Use Case Fidelity
dimensions.rationale: All 3 use_case_flows covered: sprint close documentation, mid-sprint progress snapshot, partial failure recovery.

dimensions.name: Value Clarity
dimensions.rationale: Replaces 3 manual workflows. Consistent Confluence review documents, velocity tracking with trend analysis, automated team notification.

dimensions.name: Execution Feasibility
dimensions.rationale: Jira agile REST, Confluence storage format, Slack Block Kit are all standard and well-documented. All connectors have builtin definitions.

dimensions.name: Differentiation
dimensions.rationale: Velocity trend analysis with 3-sprint rolling average, at-risk sprint detection (completion rate < 50% with > 60% elapsed), acceptance criteria extraction from descriptions.

dimensions.name: Variable Necessity
dimensions.rationale: Adoption questions cover board ID, domain, space key, parent page ID, channel, scrum master ID, and auto-close toggle. All genuinely needed.

dimensions.name: Default Quality
dimensions.rationale: Confluence parent page ID has empty default requiring mandatory user input. Board ID default (42) is a placeholder. Channel (#sprint-reviews) has a good default.

issues: No adoption_questions existed before fix - Jira board/domain, Confluence space/parent page, Slack channel all needed user-specific configuration
Cron expression 0 17 */14 * 5 for bi-weekly schedule may not fire correctly - */14 on day-of-month is unusual
Confluence parent page ID has no sensible default - user must provide before first run

template: Added 7 adoption_questions: jira_board_id, jira_domain, confluence_space_key, confluence_parent_page_id, slack_sprint_channel, scrum_master_slack_id, auto_close_sprint
